title: Senator Cortese announced new language on Monday that amends his legislation to protect people from incidents of workplace violence. Senate Bill (SB) 553 requires employers to deve
prompt_preview:

Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: According to the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), workplace violence is the second leading cause of fatal occupational injuries in the United States.
Nearly two million American workers are affected by workplace violence annually.
Senate Bill (SB) 553 requires employers to develop their own workplace violence prevention plans.
Employers must maintain a Violent Incident Log of all violent incidents against employees.
SB 553 would accelerate the creation of a workplace violence prevention standard by placing it into effect on July 1, 2024.
